other_observers: Glenn Walbek, Brandon Percival, Mark Peterson, Joey Kellner, and Duane Nelson; I was the finder of the bird, and it was identified by myself and Brandon
description: The bird was an obvious cormorant, much like the Double-crested Cormorant it was next to, with differences discussed below.   The bird was about 75 percent the size of the Double-crested Cormorant it was next to, with a slimmer body shape enhanced by the much longer tail. The overall plumage was black, with no other discernable color, perhaps due to the distance. The tail was significantly longer than the tail of the nearby Double-crested Cormorant, by at least 50-75 percent. The neck appeared a little thinner, and the bill a little smaller, but due to distance it was hard to confirm these features.Behaviors: the bird was on a stick on the middle of the reservoir, preening and just loafing around.Call: none heardPlumage: maybe adult
